content.explanation:
**Idioms, Phrases & Proverbs — Figurative Language:**

**IDIOMS** = ऐसे phrases जिनका literal (शाब्दिक) meaning कुछ और होता है
"Break a leg!" ≠ पैर तोड़ो! 😄
"Break a leg!" = Best of luck! (Good luck)

**Popular English Idioms:**
• **Hit the nail on the head** = बिल्कुल सही बात कहना
• **Under the weather** = तबियत ठीक नहीं होना
• **Bite the bullet** = मुश्किल काम हिम्मत से करना
• **Beat around the bush** = घुमा-फिराकर बात करना
• **Break the ice** = बातचीत शुरू करना
• **Burn the midnight oil** = रात को देर तक पढ़ना/काम करना
• **Call it a day** = काम बंद करना / दिन खत्म करना
• **On the fence** = undecided / neutral रहना

**PROVERBS (कहावतें):**
• Every cloud has a silver lining = हर मुश्किल में उम्मीद होती है
• Actions speak louder than words = करनी > कथनी
• Where there is a will, there is a way = मन चंगा तो कठौती में गंगा
• Don't judge a book by its cover = ऊपरी रूप से मत आँको
rules: Idioms cannot be translated literally — learn them as fixed phrases
Use idioms in appropriate contexts — they add color to speech
Idioms can be formal or informal — use carefully in professional settings
Proverbs give life lessons — use them at end of arguments/stories
Phrasal verbs are semi-idiomatic: look up, give up, put off
Learn 2-3 idioms per day and use them in conversation
Context is everything — same idiom can mean different things in different situations
content.memoryTrick:
**IDIOM = Picture the image** 🖼️
Hit the nail on head → picture someone hitting a nail perfectly
Under the weather → picture someone sick under stormy clouds
Picture बनाओ, idiom याद हो जाएगा!

examples.english: She hit the nail on the head.

examples.hindi: मुझे आज तबियत ठीक नहीं है।
examples.english: I am feeling under the weather today.

examples.hindi: रात भर जागकर उसने exams की तैयारी की।
examples.english: She burned the midnight oil for her exams.

examples.hindi: हर मुश्किल में उम्मीद होती है।
examples.english: Every cloud has a silver lining.

examples.hindi: ठीक है, आज के लिए काम बंद करते हैं।
examples.english: OK, let's call it a day.
He is still on the fence about it.

mistakes.wrong: Break the leg for good luck.
mistakes.why: "Break a leg" is the idiom — not "break the leg"

mistakes.correct: He hit the nail on the head.
mistakes.why: Idioms are fixed phrases — articles matter!

mistakes.wrong: Using idioms in formal business emails.
mistakes.correct: Avoid idioms in formal written communication.
mistakes.why: Idioms are mostly for spoken / informal contexts

speakingTips: Meetings में: 'Let us not beat around the bush — here is the real issue.'
Motivating someone: 'Break a leg in your interview tomorrow!'
Casual: 'I was burning the midnight oil last night for this presentation.'

vocabulary.example: The literal meaning is different from the idiom.
Every idiom has a specific connotation.
This is a popular English expression.

practice.hindi: वह हमेशा कठिन मेहनत करता है।
practice.english: He always burns the midnight oil.
alternatives: he always works very hard late at night.
practice.hint: Burn the midnight oil = रात भर मेहनत करना

practice.hindi: वह बातों में बहुत माहिर है।
practice.english: He has a way with words.
alternatives: he is very good at communicating.
practice.hint: Have a way with words = बोलने में माहिर होना

practice.hindi: इस काम में बहुत पैसा है।
practice.english: This work is a gold mine.
practice.hint: Gold mine = बहुत फायदेमंद

practice.hindi: उसने आखिरकार बात मान ली।
practice.english: He finally came around.
practice.hint: Come around = मान जाना/बदल जाना

practice.hindi: मेरे पास अभी समय नहीं है।
practice.english: I am tied up right now.
practice.hint: Tied up = व्यस्त होना

practice.hindi: वह अपना वादा पूरा नहीं करती।
practice.english: She always lets people down.
practice.hint: Let someone down = निराश करना

practice.hindi: वह बहुत ही खुले दिल का इंसान है।
practice.english: He is a very big-hearted person.
alternatives: he is an extremely generous person.
practice.hint: Big-hearted = उदार/दयालु

practice.hindi: इस मामले में अभी नहीं बोलना चाहिए।
practice.english: We should not let the cat out of the bag yet.
alternatives: we shouldn't reveal this secret yet.
practice.hint: Let the cat out of the bag = राज़ खोल देना

practice.hindi: वह हमेशा तुम्हारी बात काटता है।
practice.english: He always cuts you off.
practice.hint: Cut someone off = बात के बीच में काटना

practice.hindi: वह हमेशा दूसरों पर भरोसा करता है।
practice.english: He always takes things at face value.
practice.hint: Take at face value = बिना सोचे विश्वास करना

practice.hindi: अब तुम उसकी तरफ से बोल रहे हो।
practice.english: Now you are taking his side.
alternatives: now you are siding with him.
practice.hint: Take someone's side = किसी का पक्ष लेना

practice.hindi: वह मुश्किल में है।
practice.english: He is in hot water.
practice.hint: In hot water = मुसीबत में

practice.hindi: सभी सफल लोग शुरुआत में संघर्ष करते हैं।
practice.english: Every cloud has a silver lining.
alternatives: there is hope even in difficult times.
practice.hint: Every cloud has a silver lining = हर मुश्किल में उम्मीद होती है

practice.hindi: जो बोया वो काटोगे।
practice.english: You reap what you sow.
alternatives: as you sow so shall you reap.
practice.hint: You reap what you sow = करनी का फल

practice.hindi: उसने मुझे पूरी जानकारी दी।
practice.english: He filled me in on everything.
practice.hint: Fill someone in = जानकारी देना

practice.hindi: बिना परिश्रम के कुछ नहीं मिलता।
practice.english: No pain, no gain.
practice.hint: No pain no gain = मेहनत के बिना कुछ नहीं मिलता

practice.hindi: वह हमेशा काम टालता रहता है।
practice.english: He always puts things off.
practice.hint: Put off = काम को आगे टालना

practice.hindi: पहले सोचो, फिर बोलो।
practice.english: Look before you leap.
practice.hint: Look before you leap = पहले सोचो फिर करो

practice.hindi: वह हमेशा पैसे बचाता है।
practice.english: He always saves for a rainy day.
alternatives: he saves money for tough times.
practice.hint: Save for a rainy day = मुश्किल के लिए बचत करना

practice.hindi: तुम हर जगह जाते हो — घुमक्कड़ हो।
practice.english: You have itchy feet.
alternatives: you love to travel and roam.
practice.hint: Itchy feet = यात्रा करने की इच्छा

practice.hindi: इस मुश्किल में उसने मेरा साथ दिया।
practice.english: He stood by me in this difficult time.
alternatives: he supported me through this challenge.
practice.hint: Stand by someone = साथ खड़े रहना

practice.hindi: इस नौकरी में बड़ा भविष्य है।
practice.english: This job has a bright future.
alternatives: there is a lot of potential in this job.
practice.hint: Bright future = उज्जवल भविष्य

practice.hindi: वह अचानक मेरे दिमाग में आया।
practice.english: It struck me suddenly.
alternatives: the idea hit me out of the blue.
practice.hint: Strike/Hit = अचानक याद आना

practice.hindi: जल्दी का काम शैतान का।
practice.english: Haste makes waste.
practice.hint: Haste makes waste = जल्दबाज़ी में गलतियाँ होती हैं

practice.hindi: बड़ों का सम्मान करो।
practice.english: Respect your elders.
practice.hint: Respect your elders = proverb
